diff_user.system:
You are an expert data engineer specializing in converting data to text. You will be given the current Python program with its BLUE score and an inspiration program and your task will be to evolve the current Python program to improve the score. It is a program that converts a list of triples into natural language text. The given triples will be associated with the topic: University. The program must implement a function called 'predict' that accepts a list of triples and generates a coherent, contextually relevant sentence that accurately represents the information contained in the triples. Ensure that the generated text is fluent, grammatically correct, and maintains the meaning of the original data. There can be multiple triples given that make up a complex sentence. Converting all those triples into one sentence will award greater score. An example of a complex sentence:
Triples:
(Antwerp | cityServed | Antwerp International Airport)
(Belgium | country | Antwerp)
(City of Brussels | capital | Belgium)
Example sentence:
"Antwerp International Airport serves the city of Antwerp which is in Belgium, where the capital is Brussels."

The 'predict' function returns that sentence as a string. Below is the list of all possible predicates that can be given as an input. With the given predicates and example triples implement the 'predict' function so it can process all the possible predicates:

Predicate: city - Example triple: (1 Decembrie 1918 University | city | Alba Iulia)
Predicate: country - Example triple: (1 Decembrie 1918 University | country | Romania)
Predicate: latinName - Example triple: (1 Decembrie 1918 University | latinName | "Universitas Apulensis")
Predicate: nickname - Example triple: (1 Decembrie 1918 University | nickname | Uab)
Predicate: rector - Example triple: (1 Decembrie 1918 University | rector | "Breaz Valer Daniel")
Predicate: academicStaffSize - Example triple: (AWH Engineering College | academicStaffSize | 250)
Predicate: established - Example triple: (AWH Engineering College | established | 2001)
Predicate: state - Example triple: (AWH Engineering College | state | Kerala)
Predicate: governmentType - Example triple: (Aarhus | governmentType | Magistrate)
Predicate: hasToItsNortheast - Example triple: (Aarhus | hasToItsNortheast | Mols)
Predicate: dean - Example triple: (Accademia di Architettura di Mendrisio | dean | Mario Botta)
Predicate: location - Example triple: (Accademia di Architettura di Mendrisio | location | Ticino)
Predicate: numberOfStudents - Example triple: (Accademia di Architettura di Mendrisio | numberOfStudents | 600)
Predicate: affiliation - Example triple: (Acharya Institute of Technology | affiliation | Visvesvaraya Technological University)
Predicate: director - Example triple: (Acharya Institute of Technology | director | "Dr. G. P. Prabhukumar")
Predicate: motto - Example triple: (Acharya Institute of Technology | motto | "Nurturing Excellence")
Predicate: numberOfPostgraduateStudents - Example triple: (Acharya Institute of Technology | numberOfPostgraduateStudents | 700)
Predicate: numberOfUndergraduateStudents - Example triple: (Acharya Institute of Technology | numberOfUndergraduateStudents | 10000)
Predicate: officialSchoolColour - Example triple: (Acharya Institute of Technology | officialSchoolColour | "Blue, White and Orange")
Predicate: outlookRanking - Example triple: (Acharya Institute of Technology | outlookRanking | 63)
Predicate: sportsOffered - Example triple: (Acharya Institute of Technology | sportsOffered | Tennis)
Predicate: wasGivenTheTechnicalCampusStatusBy - Example triple: (Acharya Institute of Technology | wasGivenTheTechnicalCampusStatusBy | All India Council for Technical Education)
Predicate: founder - Example triple: (Bangalore | founder | Kempe Gowda I)
Predicate: leader - Example triple: (Denmark | leader | Lars Løkke Rasmussen)
Predicate: leaderTitle - Example triple: (Denmark | leaderTitle | Monarchy of Denmark)
Predicate: religion - Example triple: (Denmark | religion | Church of Denmark)
Predicate: headquarter - Example triple: (European University Association | headquarter | Brussels)
Predicate: largestCity - Example triple: (India | largestCity | Mumbai)
Predicate: river - Example triple: (India | river | Ganges)
Predicate: hasToItsWest - Example triple: (Karnataka | hasToItsWest | Arabian Sea)
Predicate: anthem - Example triple: (Romania | anthem | Deșteaptă-te, române!)
Predicate: ethnicGroup - Example triple: (Romania | ethnicGroup | Germans of Romania)
Predicate: legislature - Example triple: (Switzerland | legislature | Federal Assembly (Switzerland))
Predicate: sportGoverningBody - Example triple: (Tennis | sportGoverningBody | International Tennis Federation)
Predicate: isPartOf - Example triple: (Alba Iulia | isPartOf | Alba County)
Predicate: neighboringMunicipality - Example triple: (Mendrisio | neighboringMunicipality | Meride)

# Current Program
```python
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about universities (or related entities) into a single,
    fluent English sentence. Handles multiple triples by concatenating appropriate
    clauses.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    def phrase(t: Triple) -> str:
        pred = t.predicate.replace('_', ' ')
        # Simple heuristic phrasing for common predicates
        if pred in {"city", "country", "state", "capital", "location", "river", "largest city", "headquarter", "postal code"}:
            return f"{t.subject} is in {t.object}"
        if pred == "established":
            return f"{t.subject} was established in {t.object}"
        if pred == "rector":
            return f"The rector of {t.subject} is {t.object}"
        if pred == "dean":
            return f"The dean of {t.subject} is {t.object}"
        if pred == "president":
            return f"The president of {t.subject} is {t.object}"
        if pred == "director":
            return f"The director of {t.subject} is {t.object}"
        if pred == "nickname":
            return f"{t.subject} is nicknamed {t.object}"
        if pred == "latinName":
            return f"The Latin name of {t.subject} is {t.object}"
        if pred == "academicStaffSize":
            return f"{t.subject} has an academic staff of {t.object}"
        if pred == "numberOfStudents":
            return f"{t.subject} has {t.object} students"
        if pred == "numberOfUndergraduateStudents":
            return f"{t.subject} has {t.object} undergraduate students"
        if pred == "numberOfPostgraduateStudents":
            return f"{t.subject} has {t.object} postgraduate students"
        if pred == "numberOfDoctoralStudents":
            return f"{t.subject} has {t.object} doctoral students"
        if pred == "staff":
            return f"{t.subject} employs {t.object} staff members"
        if pred == "affiliation":
            return f"{t.subject} is affiliated with {t.object}"
        if pred == "motto":
            return f"The motto of {t.subject} is \"{t.object}\""
        if pred == "officialSchoolColour":
            return f"The official school colour of {t.subject} is {t.object}"
        if pred == "sportsOffered":
            return f"{t.subject} offers the sport {t.object}"
        if pred == "hasToItsNortheast":
            return f"{t.subject} has to its northeast {t.object}"
        if pred == "hasToItsWest":
            return f"{t.subject} has to its west {t.object}"
        if pred == "hasToItsNorthwest":
            return f"{t.subject} has to its northwest {t.object}"
        if pred == "neighboringMunicipality":
            return f"{t.subject} neighbours {t.object}"
        if pred == "isPartOf":
            return f"{t.subject} is part of {t.object}"
        # Default generic phrasing
        return f"{t.subject} {pred} {t.object}"

    clauses = [phrase(t) for t in triples]

    if len(clauses) == 1:
        return clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        # Join with commas and an 'and' before the last clause
        return ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1] + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
```

=======
def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about universities (or related entities) into a
    single fluent English sentence. The implementation groups information by
    subject and combines related predicates (like location data) into compact
    clauses, producing a more natural, concise description.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    from collections import defaultdict

    # Group triples by subject
    grouped = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        grouped[t.subject].append(t)

    # Helper maps
    location_predicates = {"city", "country", "state", "capital", "location",
                           "river", "largestCity", "headquarter", "postalCode"}
    numeric_predicates = {
        "academicStaffSize": "academic staff of",
        "numberOfStudents": "students",
        "numberOfUndergraduateStudents": "undergraduate students",
        "numberOfPostgraduateStudents": "postgraduate students",
        "numberOfDoctoralStudents": "doctoral students",
        "staff": "staff members"
    }

    def render_subject(subject: str, triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
        parts = []
        locations = []
        for t in triples:
            pred = t.predicate
            obj = t.object
            # Location related predicates are collected
            if pred in location_predicates:
                locations.append(obj)
                continue

            if pred == "established":
                parts.append(f"was established in {obj}")
            elif pred == "rector":
                parts.append(f"the rector is {obj}")
            elif pred == "dean":
                parts.append(f"the dean is {obj}")
            elif pred == "president":
                parts.append(f"the president is {obj}")
            elif pred == "director":
                parts.append(f"the director is {obj}")
            elif pred == "nickname":
                parts.append(f"is nicknamed {obj}")
            elif pred == "latinName":
                parts.append(f"has the Latin name {obj}")
            elif pred == "affiliation":
                parts.append(f"is affiliated with {obj}")
            elif pred == "motto":
                parts.append(f"its motto is \"{obj}\"")
            elif pred == "officialSchoolColour":
                parts.append(f"its official colour is {obj}")
            elif pred == "sportsOffered":
                parts.append(f"offers the sport {obj}")
            elif pred in {"hasToItsNortheast", "hasToItsWest", "hasToItsNorthwest"}:
                direction = pred.replace("hasToIts", "").lower()
                parts.append(f"lies to its {direction} {obj}")
            elif pred == "neighboringMunicipality":
                parts.append(f"neighbours {obj}")
            elif pred == "isPartOf":
                parts.append(f"is part of {obj}")
            elif pred in numeric_predicates:
                label = numeric_predicates[pred]
                if "staff" in label:
                    parts.append(f"employs {obj} {label}")
                else:
                    parts.append(f"has {obj} {label}")
            else:
                # fallback generic phrasing
                parts.append(f"{pred.replace('_', ' ')} {obj}")

        # Combine location info
        if locations:
            loc_phrase = ", ".join(locations)
            parts.insert(0, f"is located in {loc_phrase}")

        # Build clause for this subject
        clause = f"{subject} " + ", ".join(parts)
        return clause.strip()

    # Render each subject clause
    subject_clauses = [render_subject(subj, trs) for subj, trs in grouped.items()]

    # Join subject clauses into a single sentence
    if len(subject_clauses) == 1:
        sentence = subject_clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = "; ".join(subject_clauses) + "."

    return sentence
>>>>>>> REPLACE
